themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their campaigns around 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in housing, social aid, and employment. This theme is particularly prominent in Spain, France, Italy, and Norway, where parties like Vox, Rassemblement National, Fratelli d'Italia, and Fremskrittspartiet are advocating for stricter immigration controls and domestic-focused welfare reforms.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox demands 'national priority' in Andalusia elections, opposing migrant regularization and EU-Mercosur trade deals.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella advocate for economic nationalism, criticizing EU policies and prioritizing French workers.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government faces criticism for aligning with far-right rhetoric on migration and national sovereignty.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP opposes climate policies and immigration, emphasizing national economic interests and reduced bureaucracy.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage's party campaigns on anti-immigration policies, including stopping small boat crossings and deporting illegal migrants.

themes.title: Meloni’s government longevity sparks cross-national debate
themes.blurb: Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s government has become the second-longest in the country’s post-war history, sparking discussions about stability, policy achievements, and far-right governance across Europe. This theme resonates in Italy, Germany, and Hungary, where similar debates about government longevity and far-right influence are unfolding.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government surpasses Berlusconi’s tenure, facing both praise for stability and criticism for economic and social policies.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD’s rising poll numbers prompt debates about far-right governance and coalition possibilities.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungary’s political shift from Fidesz to Tisza raises questions about governance and policy continuity.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella’s leadership dynamics and potential 2027 presidential bid spark discussions about far-right governance.

themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The ongoing conflict between the U.S. and Iran under President Donald Trump continues to dominate right-wing and far-right media narratives globally. This theme is particularly strong in the U.S., UK, France, and Italy, where the war’s economic and geopolitical implications are hotly debated, often with a focus on military strategy, sanctions, and troop withdrawals.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s Iran policies, including sanctions, troop withdrawals, and military actions, dominate U.S. right-wing discourse.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage and Reform UK criticize U.S. foreign policy, including troop withdrawals from Europe and Iran strategy.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella criticize U.S. foreign policy, including Iran sanctions and their impact on France.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government faces scrutiny over its alignment with U.S. policies, including Iran sanctions and troop deployments.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox aligns with Trump’s hardline stance on Iran, criticizing EU policies and advocating for stronger sanctions.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Far-right parties across Europe are experiencing significant gains in polls and elections, reflecting a broader trend of rising populism and nationalist sentiment. This theme is evident in Germany, France, Sweden, and Norway, where parties like AfD, Rassemblement National, Sverigedemokraterna, and Fremskrittspartiet are making electoral strides.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads German polls, surpassing traditional parties and sparking debates about coalition possibilities.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella’s rising popularity ahead of the 2027 presidential election reflects far-right momentum.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD’s influence in Swedish politics grows, with debates about their role in government and impact on migration policies.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP’s strong polling and leadership under Sylvi Listhaug highlight far-right gains in Norway.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s electoral gains in local elections signal a shift in the UK’s political landscape.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric is increasingly linked to incidents of political violence and intimidation, both in physical and online spaces. This theme is particularly prominent in the U.S., UK, Italy, and Sweden, where inflammatory language from far-right leaders and media has sparked confrontations and threats.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric and policies are linked to political violence, including assassination attempts and protests.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage’s inflammatory language and Reform UK’s anti-immigration stance spark protests and altercations.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government faces criticism for far-right rhetoric, including comparisons to fascist imagery.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD’s rhetoric on migration and nationalism fuels protests and clashes with counter-demonstrators.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s inflammatory language, including insults against political opponents, sparks backlash and protests.
